Consider the following statements:
1. Vapour pressure increases on increasing temperature.
2. As temperature increases, viscosity of air decreases.
3. Dynamic viscosity of water is nearly 50 times that of air.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
As temperature increases, viscosity of air increases.

Solution

Q9 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Efficiency of a pump is generally less than that of turbine.
2. Inlet side of a pump is more susceptible to cavitation damage.
3. The losses are more in turbines than in pumps.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The losses in pumps and turbines are of same kind, but the losses in pumps are more due to eddies and turbulence.

3. If the block rises up from its original level, it is known as reverse fault.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Faults which are approximately at the right angle to the folds are called dip or cross faults.
• If one of the strips goes down with respect to its original position, it is called a normal fault.

Q105 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. In the design of a bolted joint, we have to ensure that shear strength and bearing strength of bolts are lesser than tensile strength of plate.
2. By providing proper edge distance, the value of shear stress gets decreased and shear failure of plates can be prevented.
3. The purpose of packing plate is to fill the gap, it can not take any structural load.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• In the design of a bolted joint, we have to ensure that shear strength and bearing strength of
bolts are always greater than tensile strength, of plate.
• By providing proper end distance, the value of shear stress can be decreased thereby
preventing shear failure of plates.

Q126 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

If the base period of a 4-hour unit hydrograph of a basin is 76 hours, then a 12 hour unit hydrograph derived from this 4 hour unit hydrograph will have a base period of

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The 12 hour unit hydrograph will be obtained by adding ordinates of 4 hour and ordinates of 4
hour UH lagged by 4 hours consecutively two times. So the base period for 12 hour UH will be 76
+ 4 + 4 = 84 hours.

Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Unit hydrograph theory assumes time invariance and linear response.
